description: Trigger discrimination for the Phase 6 delivery cluster: pipeline design vs release gate vs rollback authoring, and against the shipped qa-automation-architect, supply-chain-security-reviewer, and vite-build-qa-engineer.

cases.prompt: Design our CI/CD: what runs on PR vs merge vs nightly, how secrets reach deploy jobs, and where production approval sits.
cases.reason: The whole delivery pipeline — stage graph, secret governance, promotion gates — is this skill; qa-automation-architect only supplies the test-tier slice it hosts.

cases.prompt: Decide which test suites run on PR versus nightly, the sharding, and the retry policy for flaky specs.
cases.reason: Test-suite CI placement, sharding, and retry/flake policy are the automation blueprint's territory; the pipeline architect consumes that blueprint as one stage family.

cases.prompt: Audit our GitHub Actions for supply-chain risk — unpinned third-party actions, token permissions, compromise paths.
cases.reason: The CI compromise-path audit is supply-chain-security-reviewer casework; the pipeline architect APPLIES its pinning/isolation rules when building.

cases.prompt: CI is green on the release branch — are we actually ready to ship this to production?
cases.reason: A specific release's go/no-go is the readiness gate, which consumes the checks this skill designed — not a pipeline-design question.

cases.prompt: Verify our production Vite build doesn't leak any VITE_ secrets into the dist bundle before we wire it as a CI gate.
cases.reason: Running the build and proving dist-level secret absence is vite-build-qa-engineer; the pipeline architect only places that proof as a stage.
